title: podman inspect does not show network info on a joined network namespace
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On a joined network namespace mode `--net ns:<path>` podman inspect fails to report the network info like `IPAddress`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a named network namespace and setup a veth interface
```
ip netns add test
ip netns exec test /bin/bash
ip link add enp2s0 type veth peer name eth0
ip addr add 10.0.1.0/24 dev eth0
ip link set eth0 up
exit
```
2. run a container and join the created network namespace
`podman run --net ns:/run/netns/test --name test -it alpine /bin/sh`
3. check proper association `ip addr show`
```
eth0      Link encap:Ethernet  HWaddr BA:AF:09:0E:F6:96  
          inet addr:10.0.1.0  Bcast:0.0.0.0  Mask:255.255.255.0
          UP BROADCAST MULTICAST  MTU:1500  Metric:1
          RX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 frame:0
          TX packets:0 errors:0 dropped:0 overruns:0 carrier:0
          collisions:0 txqueuelen:1000 
          RX bytes:0 (0.0 B)  TX bytes:0 (0.0 B)
```
4. Check output of `podman inspect test | grep -i IPAddress`

**Describe the results you received:**

No `IPAddress` information

**Describe the results you expected:**

Expect to see the `IPAddress` of the network namespace `10.0.1.0`

**Output of `podman version`:**
`podman version 3.4.5-dev`
```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes, same defect reproducible with 4.0.0.

comments.body: This isn't possible with the current code we're using, we'd require an ability to nsenter into the container's namespace and parse the current network config; potentially a significant amount of work, there.

This sounds like a feature to me. At the moment we only use the info we have when podman configures the network via cni/netavark.

I am not sure if this would even make sense to display information on an arbitrary namespace.
First, it can have n interfaces with n ip addresses, so I have no idea what podman should display (all of them?).
Second, we do not even know how the netns is connected. It is possible that the ip address is not routable from the host.

IMO, if you use a custom netns you should not rely podman to display information about it. Since you manually configured the netns you should already know how it was configured.

We do have facilities in `podman inspect` to handle arbitrary numbers of interfaces already, so it should work - we'll display all information about the network's multiple interfaces. Agree with the rest, though - there is a lot of complexity in this request for relatively minimal benefit. I'm not opposed to merging a fix but I don't see the core Podman team working on it in the near future.

Thanks for the prompt answer and transparent info.
I hoped it is a low-hanging fruit which could be easily fixed by just collecting the network config via netns but looking around in podman code-base and your input it seems not to be the case.
I will try to give it more in-depth analysis if a relatively small patch is feasible without causing so much ripples otherwise I would not oppose to be postponed or eventually closed.

@mheon I opened up a PR to add the missing inspect info, but the pipeline seems to fail due to other reasons, TBH IDK whether podman CI is reliable at all. What is the right course of action here? Please help me out
